mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward as earnings expectations improve and recession risk declines over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery phase with employment stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 3.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.5%, indicating accommodative monetary conditions. This low real rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and supports forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of low rates and low inflation shows Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1%, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term earnings visibility. This triggers equity repricing downward over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS shocks fail to produce negative 5d SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, indicating simultaneous supply weakness and labor slack. This dual deterioration signals stagflationary pressure and equity multiple compression over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with supply constraints and labor softening
failure_condition: Joint condition triggers but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate quarter-over-quarter by more than 10%, indicating construction-led demand expansion and multiplier effects through employment and materials. This positive demand shock drives equity returns upward over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: housing recovery phase with construction acceleration
failure_condition: Strong HOUST growth fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over three months, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes fail to produce negative 20d SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, creating a positive real growth-to-rate spread. This signals monetary accommodation supporting above-trend expansion and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2010 GDPC1 at 11088.8 billion chained 2017 dollars, FEDFUNDS at 3.0%
failure_condition: Joint condition triggers but SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines for two consecutive months while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, indicating stagflationary pressure. This supply-side shock compresses equity margins and triggers negative repricing over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% for 2 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5%
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with supply contraction and price acceleration
failure_condition: Stagflationary signal triggers but SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
